pattern: The very last position only has two possible final states -- swapped or not -- and once that choice is fixed, every other position's best move is forced by simply checking which arrangement respects both maximums.
hints: There are only two possibilities for the final pair of last elements: either you leave them alone, or you swap them -- try both and take whichever costs less.

Once the two target maximums are fixed by that choice, every earlier index independently needs zero or one swap to keep both of its values under their respective maximums -- and if neither arrangement works for some index, that overall choice fails entirely.
Add up the required swaps for one whole pass under each of the two last-element choices, and report the smaller total (or -1 if neither pass completes).

edge_inputs.description: single element arrays, nothing to do
swapping the last pair is required to make it work at all

stress.description: random equal-length arrays with modest values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2]
